title: orobanche minor subsp. maritima
intro: Orobanche minor subsp. maritima, also known as the maritime broomrape, is a parasitic plant species that belongs to the Orobanchaceae family. It is known to contain medicinal properties, including alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which have been studied for their potential health benefits. The maritime broomrape has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal issues, and has been found to exhibit antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties. Orobanche minor subsp. maritima can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or capsules, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
